cards.back: An algorithm used to identify whether a deadlock exists in a system by checking for a set of processes that are holding resources and waiting for others
A deadlock detection and prevention algorithm that uses a set of banks to manage resources, and checks for safety and safety conditions to prevent deadlocks
A process that requests a resource from another process and waits for it to be released, causing a deadlock situation
A process of recovering from a deadlock situation by aborting a process, releasing a resource, or using other recovery mechanisms
A scheduling algorithm that divides the CPU time into fixed intervals, allowing for efficient deadlock detection and resolution by interrupting the process and allocating resources
